title: Traveling with an electric car
body:
Two years ago we decided to get rid of our old VW Polo. The [Polín II](https://www.instagram.com/p/CNQNyeVjZNm/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link) served us well for over 10 years and almost 200,000km (we bought it for €4,000 with 90,000km and 8 years old).

We had wanted to do without owning a car for some time. In Vila de Gràcia it's more of a nuisance than useful (parking, insurance, maintenance, etc.), and in cities like Barcelona you don't need a car since there are many other alternatives: besides Public Transport we have services like *[b](https://www.bicing.barcelona/)[icing](https://www.bicing.barcelona/)*, *moto sharing*, *car sharing* as well as traditional car rentals for getaways.

In anticipation, we were already members of [SomMobilitat](https://www.sommobilitat.coop), a car-sharing cooperative founded in Mataró and operating throughout Catalonia. There are 6 cars available at 3 [BSM parking lots](https://twitter.com/xarop/status/1565763503629254657) near our home and it's our option when we go on excursions and weekend family outings, in case we need to complement public transport.

Last summer we already did a [short getaway](https://www.instagram.com/p/CSoUnmZDGcw/?utm_source=ig_web_copy_link) with a [Renault Zoe](https://www.renault.es/electricos/zoe.html) from SomMobilitat. We stayed around home, the Pyrenees and Costa Brava; it was our first experience of taking a short trip with an electric car and it went quite well.

Given that experience, this year we decided to take a longer trip in time and distance. A few days in the Pyrenees and then a long week through France, Brittany and Perigord.

Before heading to Brittany I planned the route thanks to the [A Better Router Planner](https://abetterrouteplanner.com/) (ABRP) app. By entering origin, destination and car characteristics it suggests stops. I also checked that these chargers were compatible with the [Electromaps key](https://www.electromaps.com/ca-ES/electropass) which is what SomMobilitat offers you. Car rental includes charging if you do it with this device; if you rent the car for more than 5 days you can use the [long-term rate](https://www.sommobilitat.coop/tarifa-llarga-durada/), the daily price is reduced and you pay per km traveled.

With the same Zoe as last year we left on August 23rd at 5:00am with 100% battery. First stop for breakfast and to charge the car at [Village Catalan](https://goo.gl/maps/pjGuNywXubJisVVH9), near Perpignan, already in France. In less than 1h we had enough time to enjoy the sunrise and "fill the tank" from 25 to 95% at an [Ionity](https://ionity.eu/) fast charger.

> [
> 
> View this post on Instagram
> 
> ](https://www.instagram.com/p/ChlxWhZIIvZ/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)
> 
> [A post shared by diluvi (@diluvi)](https://www.instagram.com/p/ChlxWhZIIvZ/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)

As the [ABRP](https://abetterrouteplanner.com/?plan_uuid=ee0d3038-4a37-43b6-9a20-88b9435d8fcf) app indicated, near Toulouse we made a short 2nd stop to charge from 40 to 75% and, at a [TotalEnergies](https://www.totalenergies.fr/) service area on the outskirts of Bordeaux, the 3rd stop of the day, 5 minutes queue because all the "pumps" were occupied and 1 hour to charge up to 95%, time used to have lunch and stretch our legs.

> [
> 
> View this post on Instagram
> 
> ](https://www.instagram.com/p/Chmj3PSoCvU/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)
> 
> [A post shared by diluvi (@diluvi)](https://www.instagram.com/p/Chmj3PSoCvU/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)

> The service areas and rest zones on French highways are large and very well equipped, clean bathrooms and tables for picnicking in the shade of the trees.

The last stretch was direct to our intermediate destination, a hotel very close to the center of [La Rochelle](https://www.larochelle-turismo.es/). We arrived with 15% battery and with the peace of mind that we could charge the car at the hotel during the night.

> [
> 
> View this post on Instagram
> 
> ](https://www.instagram.com/p/ChokG0uo4rG/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)
> 
> [A post shared by diluvi (@diluvi)](https://www.instagram.com/p/ChokG0uo4rG/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)

Just 12h to cover more than 800km from Barcelona, including stops to charge the car and our meal breaks, we arrived well rested to be tourists and have dinner with the traditional mussels and chips from the area.

Without getting up too early and after breakfast we headed to our main destination, Brittany, with a single 40-minute stop near Nantes we had enough battery to reach the [Quiberon peninsula](https://www.vacaciones-bretana.com/destinos/10-destinos/el-sur-de-bretana-el-golfo-del-morbihan/la-peninsula-de-quiberon/).

Once in Brittany the trips were short and we took the opportunity to charge the car while we visited the villages and tourist areas, we had no difficulty finding chargers in public parking lots.

> [
> 
> View this post on Instagram
> 
> ](https://www.instagram.com/p/Ch1hM3coFJk/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)
> 
> [A post shared by diluvi (@diluvi)](https://www.instagram.com/p/Ch1hM3coFJk/?utm_source=ig_embed&utm_campaign=loading)

A week visiting picturesque villages and beaches, the weather was with us, swimming in the Atlantic and changing landscapes due to the effects of the tides.

The return was similar, we made the intermediate stop in the [Perigord](https://www.guide-du-perigord.com/) area, we took advantage of the evening and morning to visit the area and taste the typical gastronomy.

We left after lunch with 100% battery, with 2 stops of less than an hour at Ionity points which were the most reliable, we had enough to arrive in Barcelona with 15% battery.

![](http://www.xarop.com/dev/wp-content/uploads/2022/09/IMG_20220902_133045471-1280x960.jpg)
> Zoe 50 Statistics

I tried to be efficient with driving, always in eco mode, not exceeding 100Km/h and taking advantage of regenerative braking and driving on secondary roads to better enjoy the landscapes and consume less, except for the long initial and final stretches which we did on the highway where the infrastructure for fast chargers is located.

## Conclusions

It is completely feasible to take a trip like this, at least in France, at home we've had more problems, the infrastructure is not yet at the level of other European countries.

And that's with the Zoe which doesn't allow charges of more than 50kWh. With cars that allow charges up to 250kWh the trip would have been similar to one with combustion, faster. But, my conclusion is that it's more pleasant to travel without rushing.
